steps.title: Identify the timeline signal
steps.content: The phrase “Before that” is the key. “That” refers to when he watched North by Northwest at last year's festival and wrote the scathing review. “Before that” tells us we're describing what was true at an earlier point in the past.

steps.title: Apply the tense rule for prior past events
steps.content:
When a past event must be shown as occurring before another past event, use past perfect (had + been). “Had been” correctly establishes that A Beautiful Mind was already his least-enjoyed film before he watched North by Northwest.

The other choices either use present tense forms or express an ongoing action rather than an established prior fact.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
